chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No.D- 434 of 2024 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of Case 1. For orders on office objections 2. For hearing of main case 10-09-2024 Miss Farzana (Bhatti) Makwal, Advocate for petitioner. Mr. Ghulam Abbas Kubar, Assistant A.G a/w Riaz Ahmed Bhutto, Assistant Litigation Officer, Survey Superintendent Sukkur Division at Khairpur. -.-.-.-.-.-.- Chairman, Municipal Committee, Rohri is present and states that Chief Officer, District Council, Sukkur may be called. Let notice be issued to him for the next date of hearing to be present in person. Show-cause notice issued against Chairman, Municipal Committee, Rohri stands discharged. Statement is also filed by Survey Superintendent, Sukkur Division at Khairpur to show that in Survey No.132, there is a graveyard admeasuring 05 acres; however, no boundary thereof has been marked. Since this is a land which forms part of a larger survey so probably private individuals are encroaching upon the graveyard’s portion. The Survey Superintendent is directed to mark boundaries of the graveyard through the help of concerned Assistant Commissioner and Mukhtiarkar as well as ensure that within the land designated for the graveyard, no sale and purchase transaction to take place nor any one shall encroach upon the said land. This Court’s attention is drawn to the order dated 26.05.2021 passed in C.P.No.D-1294 of 2016, whereby Court directed the concerned Deputy Commissioners to remove all encroachments from the graveyards and to demarcate the lands of graveyards as well as construct boundary walls in order to ensure that the graveyards in Sindh are preserved.

The Survey Superintendent is directed to mark boundaries of the graveyard through the help of concerned Assistant Commissioner and Mukhtiarkar as well as ensure that within the land designated for the graveyard, no sale and purchase transaction to take place nor any one shall encroach upon the said land. This Court’s attention is drawn to the order dated 26.05.2021 passed in C.P.No.D-1294 of 2016, whereby Court directed the concerned Deputy Commissioners to remove all encroachments from the graveyards and to demarcate the lands of graveyards as well as construct boundary walls in order to ensure that the graveyards in Sindh are preserved. The Senior Member Board of Revenue was also directed to ensure that no 2 fresh mutation entries in respect of all the graveyards throughout Sindh Province be kept in the revenue record. Let Deputy Commissioner, Sukkur be present on the next date of hearing to apprise this Court as to how this Court’s order referred hereinabove has been implemented and ensure that the subject graveyard is also preserved by erecting boundary wall once demarcation of the same is made. Let needful be done before next date of hearing. Adjourned to 15.10.2024. Office to provide a copy of this order to the office to AAG. J U D G E J U D G E Ahmad
